summary.intent: Parse entire CLI JSONL log files into structured database tables instead of relying on direct Claude Code hook payloads, enabling full conversation/event history to be queried.
summary.outcome: Added 5 new Prisma tables (LogEvent, LogContent, LogUsage, LogHookProgress, LogSystemEvent) and an indexFullJsonl() function that successfully parsed 45 checkpoints into 21,862 total rows across the new tables.

repo: The handler CLI supports an 'index-checkpoints' command with --dir and --db flags
Schema is managed via Prisma at /Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/prisma/schema.prisma
JSONL event types: assistant, user, progress, system; progress events have nested data.type subtypes; user messages can contain tool_result content blocks
The indexer uses pre-fetched uuid/messageId sets for O(1) deduplication before inserting rows

code.finding: New exported function indexFullJsonl(db, transcriptPath, sessionLinkId?) dispatches per-event-type to create LogContent/LogUsage, LogHookProgress, or LogSystemEvent child rows
indexFullJsonl is called automatically from indexCheckpointV2 after each SessionLink is created, linking log events back to their checkpoint session
LogEvent holds one row per JSONL line with common fields plus raw data JSON and FK to SessionLink; child tables hold type-specific normalized fields

workflow: db:push required --accept-data-loss flag to apply schema changes that dropped/altered columns
TypeScript type checking with npx tsc --noEmit confirmed no errors before running migrations
Used Python one-liners extensively to explore JSONL structure before writing TypeScript parsing code

friction: Initial db:push failed without --accept-data-loss flag, requiring a second attempt
User interrupted the first tool call (Read of schema.prisma + Glob of src) before providing the example JSONL path, requiring workflow restart
Multiple exploratory Bash/Python commands were needed to discover JSONL event structure before schema design could begin
open_items: LogEvent.data stores raw JSON blob — individual progress data subtypes beyond hookEvent/hookName/command are not further normalized
No index/query examples or API endpoints were created to actually consume the new log tables
Deduplication relies on pre-fetched sets in memory; behavior on very large JSONL files (memory pressure) is untested
The SessionLink relation on LogEvent is optional (sessionLinkId?) — orphaned log events with no checkpoint link are possible
